sentences.text: Hi, Patrick.
Hey, how you guys doing?
Good, how are you?
Good, good.
This should be a pretty short meeting.
I just had some questions I needed to kind of… pick you guys' brain.
I'm not…
Okay.
white, well, I'm seeing what I'm seeing, okay?
Let me just…
summer.
So, can you, bring up your, the market survey template.
I want to take a look at, So, let's take a look at, I'm working on… let's look at, High Point, or San… actually, San Mateo.
And I just need to get some clarification on some things here.
Yep.
So…
One particular thing I noticed was the… when you guys say on your calculator, calculated matrix, where it says, least.
Are you guys counting for the current occupancy, including the pre-leasing numbers on that number?
Is that the combination of those two?
Yeah, so pre-leasing, remove… well, let me toggle that on real quick, just so we can look at everything.
Beautiful.
Okay.
So, occupant… so I'll kind of get on the list here.
So, occupant… occupancy is literally everything that is… that is occupied.
lease percentage will include everything that's occupied, plus has, it'll also include vacant, or I'm sorry, yeah, vacant leased.
Yeah.
Okay, so, like, what's nice here is, like, we're showing… you know, a 0.
3% difference, so this isn't probably a great one, but if you click into vacant, we can probably… there's probably one unit, if we're gonna look here.
Yeah, so this one unit right here is being counted into the least percentage number that is not being factored into the occupancy percentage number.
So that's the small delta with least an occupancy percentage.
Okay.
Command.
Yeah, for pre-leasing, we're removing any units that are on notice.
Okay.
So that's kind of the three differences, you know, there.
Okay.
And here's what I would say, like, what's nice about this is you obviously have your own internal numbers there.
You can match everything up, so if you click into the vacant or available units, you can kind of see the status for everything.
Correct.
what I would say is… Since we're not… or since we're tracking all this information with publicly available data, and we're not plugged into, like, your property management software.
Internally, with us, you know, for us to move a unit from an application to a lease phase, we have to wait 7 days.
We just have a 7-day grace period.
So internally, you guys might move a unit from an application stage to a lease stage in 2 days, or 3 days, or 4 days.
So I think that could be the very small delta that we're seeing there, that, like, 1%, and I think, the one instance you sent over.
But again, what's nice about this is you can drill into there and see which, in this instance, we have 3 units that are under applications.
We can see those three specific units.
Right.
Does that make sense?
I'll explain that?
I think, yeah, it makes sense, I guess, let me ask you… let me see… Provides, amenities.
In regards to… also, in regards to definitions, if you hover over any of those terms, it does give you a little… how we're calculating that.
Yeah.
It's interesting, on my… where does the… Advertised occupancy numbers show up on my… summary, because I'm not seeing… I see the lease percentage, but I'm not seeing the advertised occupancy.
Is there certain metrics I gotta select on the customized.
So, yeah, if you're looking at the screen there, there'll be this customized metric drop-down.
Yeah.
And you can see there's… these are kind of the headline… or the headers of the reports, but if you go to calculated metrics, you toggle on whatever you want.
Yeah.
And then what I… whenever you kind of… and you can see, like, some of these metrics are over different time horizons.
Correct.
Whenever you settle in on whatever metrics you prefer.
I have… I always set this up the way that I like it.
But whenever you have it settled in on what you like, click this bottom left-hand button, and that'll apply those metrics to all of your market surveys.
Yeah, yeah.
So…

I think that's where some of my team members are getting confused, too, because I think they're… because they do all… they confirm these numbers, they also… they just don't go by the trust but verify, you know?
Makes sense.
are sent to our… all these, reports are sent to our partners as well.
Yeah.
So they… and trust me, they…
They have their own.

things are constantly, and, you know, my thing is, you know, they… They have different, you know, they have different management companies they work with, and, you know, we're owner-operator, but we do have equity partners who also own other properties in other markets.
For sure.

Yeah, I would say, I mean, if yourself, your team, any investors have any questions at all, I mean, reach out, because kind of like I was showing you, we can trace all this information back to the source.
We can at least kind of follow the life cycle of an available unit, and we can figure out where that discrepancy might be coming from.
Yeah, yeah, I think that's… that's been the… and a lot of them have access to CoStar as well, so they're looking at, you know, they're checking, verifying.

We have access to it, I have access to CoStar as well, so it's kind of like there's not exact… I mean, the… what they're telling you in person when you call, and what they're telling you… what you're seeing on… online, is there's so many, like, there's so many different factors, I just want to make sure that we're all on the team is looking at the right numbers, because I think there's some confusion On that, I did Patrick these numbers, and they were calling me, saying these numbers aren't right.
I mean, well, I said, well, the CoStar number is this, and you're… the site employees telling them a different number.
Yeah.
So that's… there's some time gap between that, so I just wanted to… somewhere in the middle is the… That's how I.
Right?
It's not exact science, somewhere in the middle is the more accurate number.
For sure.
Yeah, I would just say, in regards to CoStar, we're gonna be more accurate and up-to-date than CoStar every time.
I think their data's, like, 4 to 6 weeks lagging.
Yeah, I think CoStar does it, they update it.
Well, they told me that they update it every week, so I don't know if that's accurate, you know?
I did it every week for their Apartments.
com users, so for those sites that aren't on Apartments.
com, they have a call service that calls into these markets once a month, so…
Yeah, yeah, and that's where I was like, that's… that's some… there's some lag there as well.
Yeah.
So let me ask you a question, because…
Couple of our…
equity partners have some reservations about an IQ.
I think you guys, they might… they might have some experience in the past with it.
And they, you know, they're very, very keen on.
They're like, Patrick, please just make sure that… someone's actually quantifying these numbers in person, you know, calling these sites and getting numbers.

And now, of course, with the whole real pay, you already nonsense, and a lot of companies are staying away from putting any kind of number out there for any kind of liability, in which I think it's silly, personally, but it is what it is.
But that's something that's been on the radar, so I just want to make sure when they ask me questions, that I'm actually informed, too.
push back on them, saying, no, this number's pretty accurate, checked on this number.
Sites teams are calling the sites, or are actually still sharing numbers with us.
You know, that's… I think that's very pivotal for us.
So that's what we're kind of looking at right now.
Yeah, makes sense.
Yeah, and to your point, I've got a list of, like, 15 of the top 25 PMCs that have instructed their site teams to not participate.
Yeah.
I think, I mean, I think it's gonna be harder and harder to get that information.
Yeah.
And then I… Go ahead.

I was gonna say, I mean, along the same lines, too, like, you call those pro- and this is me playing devil's advocate, I'm sorry, but, you call those properties, you're talking to, like, a 20-year-old leasing agent a lot of the times, and they're saying, hey, you know, we're at 95% occupancy.
Then you jump on their website, and they're offering 2 months free.
So, a lot of that stuff's… I don't know, what I like about this, and again, I'm biased, but every data point metric calculation, every concession, I can trace it all back to the source.
So, like, if you say, hey, like, where is this occupancy percentage coming from?
I can tell you exactly how I'm calculating, I can show you the data behind it, as opposed to just saying, hey, you know, we're at 95%, or we're at 98%, so…
Yeah, yeah.
No, I think that's… I think that's pivotal, right now.
I think that's what we're looking at.
So, you know, this is new to a lot of the site personnel, including myself.

So, you know, it's like, you know, everyone's saying, hey, these numbers aren't right, people are kind of Not freaking out, but they're kind of like, what is the So that's been… that's the reason I wanted to have this quick phone call, because I needed to understand where these numbers are coming from, how they're… how you guys are getting used, with the exposure.
Now, the exposure number, is that… That is your leases that are on notice?
Is that how you guys… how are you guys getting that number on the expense?
Exposure's ATR, just your available to rent there.
Okay, that's all you guys are looking at, okay?
Yep.
Yeah, I mean, we have this, this Help Center here, too, and if you click into it, we have this glossary.
Yeah, yeah.
It literally breaks down every calculation in there.
Yeah, no, I looked at that, and it was helpful.
I have… trust me, I had, like, a laundry list of questions, so I was able to go in there and look, get some of the answers, questions answered.
Yeah, and I think the main thing is us just getting around our head around this, and my site team's getting their head around this… the software.
I told them it's a tool.
It's not… it's the end-all, be-all, it's a tool that they need to… but they still need to quantify these numbers in person, and whoever's still sharing information with you, share information.
It is what it is, it's the best number we can get, you know.
I would ask, too, I mean, for those sites that are still sharing, if there is a discrepancy, maybe be like, hey, you know, we're showing you guys at 93%, you know, you're at 95%.
I mean, kind of asking those questions and making sure they are telling the truth.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
But I would say, too, if you.

If you have any LPs that, like, want a walkthrough, I'm happy to walk them through it and explain everything from our end, and hopefully, you know, just a different perspective in terms of explaining the data.
Yeah, yeah, one particular guy, I think I've… I think I've settled his nerves.
Yeah.

Because he, he, he just had a bad experience with it, I think, and he's a VP at an asset management company, I'm not gonna tell you who, but, he had a bad experience with it, with you guys, and he just said, he was, like, we were like, oh, God, do you have to use it, Patrick?
And I'm like.
So we're kind of, you know, trying them out right now.
I think it's, you know, it saves a lot of time for my site staff.
My site people are literally on the phone all day.
getting data, and going to all the websites, getting data, because they've got a very… they don't trust half the people.
There's some managers that are very honest, and they tell you, but other 50% either are being dishonest, or just… They just don't know, they just say that number, so…
Yeah, for sure.
Okay.

Yeah, and if that asset manager used this in the past, I mean, it's one of those things where I would say, even over the last 2 years, three years, I mean, it's night and day difference compared to where we were 5 years ago.
Yeah.
AI helped out a lot.

I mean, we've got something like 350 unique scrapers now that we use to account for, like, every combination of management software, leasing portal, you know, those ingrains and everything now, so the data, I mean, it should be a night and day, you know, difference in terms of experience, hopefully.
So, I mean, like I said, it seems a little more accurate than the… and that's what I told him, I said, and he's like, are people… are your people still calling people?
I'm like, yes, we're still calling people, okay?
Who's willing to share the information?
Yes.
Other people aren't willing to?
Right.
for whatever reason, I… we know, because the whole, you know, Justice Department investigation, all that nonsense.
Yeah.
That was way blown out of whey proportion, I don't understand that, but it is what it is.
I'm with you.
Anyways, no, that's it, guys.

That's all I wanted to just kind of go over these numbers and just kind of see where you guys got something out, like, because a lot of it was already, filled out by me when, Jamie sent me the information for the help desk, so that all the questions were asked.
Awesome.
You guys answered.
So, if anything else pops up from left field, I'll go there first.
If I'm not getting the answer I need, I'll reach out to you, both of you.
Awesome.
Okay.
Yeah, and like I said, any other… anybody else that wants a walkthrough or a quick call like this, we're happy to do it.
Yeah, yeah.
No, I think, for the most part, I'm the one who deals with most of our partners.
Mike, you know, Mike and I deal with more of our partners and stuff like that, so that's, I think, as long as I got the information and data correct, and the messages, you know.
gotta get those clarifications, that's all I need, you know.
You know, so… No, I appreciate your time.
Thanks so much, guys.
Okay?
Thanks a lot.
Have a good day, okay?
Yeah, perfect.
Alright, take care.
Bye-bye.
